title: Angel Types Rebuild Spoilers
wikitext:
[]: Angel Types. (Rebuild Spoilers) Post by CyberXIII » Sun Mar 21, 2010 8:54 pm In the manga, Kaworu mentions that Armisael is an "encroachment type". When I was thinking about the rest of the Angels, I realized that once you get past the odd shapes, they do seem to fit into certain roles. Adam and Lillith are in their own category for obvious reasons. They're basically gods, or Seeds. Sachiel, Shamshel, Zeruel, Israfel, and the two new guys fit into the "autonomous weapon" type that Gendo mentions in an early episode. They each have the same overall body plan of humankind (bilateral symmetry, bipedal body plan, etc.) Gaghiel, Sandolphon, Matariel, Sahaquiel and Arael fit into a different type. They seem suited towards extreme environments. Matarael and Gaghiel are suited to crossing water, Sandy fits in volcanic environments, and Arael exists in space. I call this the "Extraterrestrial" type. (Go ahead and laugh) Ireul, Armisael, and Bardiel fit into the "encroachment" type. They seek to parasitically invade whatever their target was. Ramiel and Leliel fit into their own category since they make no biological sense whatsoever. Leliel is a floating black hole. Ramiel is a floating fortress. Therefore, I call this the "Bizarre" type. Tabris is the closest thing the angels get to human. He's in a class of his own: the Lilin type. Do any of these groupings make sense, or do any of you have better ideas?

[]: Post by Sachi » Sun Mar 21, 2010 9:03 pm I think Shamshel is the least "humanoid" of that category you've stuck it in. It's a lot like a bug, similar to Matarael. I believe that's my only qualm with your categorization.

[]: Post by Reichu » Sun Mar 21, 2010 9:28 pm Sachi wrote: I think Shamshel is the least "humanoid" of that category you've stuck it in.

[]: Post by CyberXIII » Sun Mar 21, 2010 9:49 pm True. I was really talking about the fact that they're all walking tanks, more or less. They all use brute force in combat rather than any sort of trick. I probably should have made that more clear....although now that I think about it, the new guys could go better with Gaghiel and co.

[]: Post by NemZ » Mon Mar 22, 2010 8:37 am I don't think anything useful will come of this because each angel is too unique. You might as well try and fit each angel into a D&D or Final Fantasy class. ...And now I have the mental image of Sahaquiel as a dragoon.

[]: Post by AuraTwilight » Mon Mar 22, 2010 12:38 pm I think Kaworu was just making an observation about the Angel's method of attack. This isn't Pokemon.

[]: Post by Reichu » Mon Mar 22, 2010 2:49 pm AuraTwilight wrote: This isn't Pokemon.

[]: Post by Joseph the PRPD » Mon Mar 22, 2010 2:51 pm Reichu wrote: Ireul - ???

[]: Post by AuraTwilight » Mon Mar 22, 2010 6:48 pm Ireul is totally Missingno, and thus a Bird-type.

[]: Post by Sachi » Mon Mar 22, 2010 6:55 pm Reichu wrote: Clockiel and Tunniel aren't too humanoid, either.

[]: Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Tue Mar 23, 2010 11:24 am While they may not have "types" I do think that many of the Angels play similar narrative roles. CyberXIII wrote: Ireul, Armisael, and Bardiel fit into the "encroachment" type. They seek to parasitically invade whatever their target was.

[]: Post by planet news » Tue Mar 23, 2010 3:41 pm Sachi wrote: I didn't mention those two because I've yet to see 2.0.

[]: Post by Ramiel » Thu Apr 08, 2010 7:30 am Reichu wrote: Ireul - ???

[]: Post by TheLobe » Thu Apr 22, 2010 7:03 am kaworu's like hydrogen

[]: Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Thu Apr 22, 2010 1:22 pm Someday we will have a serious discussion thread about Angel classification and categorisation. Unfortunately, this is not that thread.
